outcome.headline: Agent timed out after 7 sequential 10-minute FastText retrainings; best model 161MB/0.6178 missed both 150MB size cap and 0.62 accuracy gate; never used model.quantize().
outcome.what_verifier_checked: fasttext test /app/model.bin /tests/private_test.txt yields P@1>=0.62 AND os.path.getsize('/app/model.bin') < 150*1024*1024
outcome.what_agent_produced: A 161MB FastText supervised model with P@1=0.6178 (last training cut off by AgentTimeoutError at 7200s); both constraints violated.
outcome.exact_failure_quote:
-rw-rw-rw- 1 root root 161M May 21 03:38 /app/model.bin
Warning : `load_model` does not return WordVectorModel or SupervisedModel any more, but a `FastText` object which is very similar.
Test samples: 10000
Precision@1: 0.6178
Recall@1: 0.6178

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent hyperparameter search until timeout
failure_modes.description: Agent ran 7 sequential ~10-min train_supervised iterations sweeping dim/bucket/epoch on a 1-CPU box, each time shrinking capacity to fight size and then trying to recover accuracy; never satisfied both constraints and was cut off by the 7200s agent timeout mid-eighth run.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 7200.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Missed fasttext quantization API
failure_modes.description: Agent treated the <150MB constraint as a capacity-reduction problem and kept lowering dim/bucket (costing accuracy), instead of using FastText's standard model.quantize(retrain=True) which compresses a trained model ~10x while preserving accuracy and would have satisfied both constraints in one step from the dim=100 baseline.

counterfactual.X: After the first 1.4GB / 0.6115 baseline at step 55-58, call model.quantize(input='data/train.txt', retrain=True, qnorm=True, cutoff=100000) and save the .ftz model as /app/model.bin
counterfactual.Y: Quantized fasttext supervised models typically drop ~10x in size while losing <1pt accuracy, simultaneously hitting size<150MB and accuracy>=0.62 in one additional step instead of 7 capacity-shrinking retrains.

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent trained a real fasttext supervised model on the provided yelp data using legitimate parameters; no attempt to read /tests/private_test.txt or game the verifier.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier loads private test file at runtime from /tests/private_test.tar.gz extracted at verification time and checks fasttext CLI accuracy plus file size; agent cannot trivially game either check.

notes_for_aggregation: Compute-bound long-horizon ML task on 1 CPU: each train_supervised run ~10 min, leaving room for only ~7 trials inside the 2-hour agent timeout. Failure is the combination of an unknown high-leverage API (model.quantize) and a slow trial-and-error loop that cannot afford to be wrong many times.
